name: Napoleons
recipeInstructions:
1. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pastry to 1/16-inch thick.
2. Transfer the pastry to a parchment-lined sheet pan and trim edges. Prick the surface all over with a fork and chill for 30 minutes.
3. Pre-heat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Cover the pastry with a second piece of parchment paper and weight it down with a second sheet pan. Bake for 10 minutes. Remove the top sheet pan and parchment, then continue baking for another 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Transfer the pastry to a cooling rack.
4. Make the filling:
- In a heavy saucepan, combine the heavy cream, milk, and sugar. Scrape the seeds of the vanilla bean into the milk mixture and add the whole bean. Bring to a boil, then remove from heat and steep for one hour. Discard the bean or reserve it for another use.
5. Pre-heat the oven to 300°F (150°C). Whisk the yolks into the cooled milk mixture and strain through a fine sieve into a 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Place the dish in a larger roasting pan and add enough hot water to reach halfway up the side of the dish. Bake until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean, then let cool; cover and chill for several hours or overnight.
6. To assemble:
- Transfer the pastry to a flat surface. Using a ruler, measure out three 3-1/2 x 11-inch strips. Carefully cut the pastry using a gentle sawing motion.
- Cut the custard into two 3-1/2 x 11-inch strips and cut each strip in half crosswise. With a long, wide offset spatula, transfer a piece of custard onto a strip of pastry, covering half of it. Transfer a second piece of custard to fill the first layer. Top with a second strip of pastry, smooth side up, and repeat the process. If necessary, smooth the sides of the napoleon with a knife so that they are stacked neatly.
7. Carefully slice the napoleon into individual serving pieces. Sift confectioner's sugar over the napoleon to form a thick coating. Heat skewers on a stovetop burner until red-hot and brand the sugar with cross-hatch marks.
